title: Stuck at created container after error message: WARN[0001] Failed to add conmon to cgroupfs sandbox cgroup: mkdir /sys/fs/cgroup/systemd/libpod_parent: permission denied
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Creating a container with a rootless user, **with portmapping** results in being stuck at started container. However I used the same packages in my working setup for RHEL 7.6
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Install Centos 7.6 and then install packages through rpm:
- conmon-1.12.0-12.dev.gitc4f232a.fc29.x86_64
- runc-1.0.0-92.dev.gitc1b8c57.fc31.x86_64
- slirp4netns-0.3.0-1.1.x86_64
- podman-1.2.0-24.dev.git0458daf.fc31.x86_64
- shadow-utils46-newxidmap-4.6-4.el7.x86_64 (Fork for newuid)

2. Enable user namespaces
cat <<EOT > /etc/sysctl.d/51-rootless.conf
user.max_user_namespaces = 15076
EOT
sysctl --system

3. Add users to subuid  subgid
cat <<EOT > /etc/subuid
testaccount:100000:65536
EOT
cp /etc/subuid subgid -y

4. Login to testaccount and start NGINX container:
testaccount: podman --log-level run -d -p 80 nginx

**Describe the results you received:**
[testaccount@centos76 ~]$ podman --log-level=debug run -d -p 80 nginx
INFO[0000] running as rootless                          
DEBU[0000] Initializing boltdb state at /home/testaccount/.local/share/containers/storage/libpod/bolt_state.db 
DEBU[0000] Using graph driver vfs                       
DEBU[0000] Using graph root /home/testaccount/.local/share/containers/storage 
DEBU[0000] Using run root /tmp/1000                     
DEBU[0000] Using static dir /home/testaccount/.local/share/containers/storage/libpod 
DEBU[0000] Using tmp dir /run/user/1000/libpod/tmp      
DEBU[0000] Using volume path /home/testaccount/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes 
DEBU[0000] Set libpod namespace to ""                   
DEBU[0000] Not configuring container store              
DEBU[0000] Using slirp4netns netmode                    
INFO[0000] running as rootless                          
DEBU[0000] Initializing boltdb state at /home/testaccount/.local/share/containers/storage/libpod/bolt_state.db

DEBU[0000] /usr/libexec/podman/conmon messages will be logged to syslog 
DEBU[0000] running conmon: /usr/libexec/podman/conmon    args=[-c 6dbd08b69aa189466ff8f248e20ca7efb55caf751fec6eef1e16c300548cbf85 -u 6dbd08b69aa189466ff8f248e20ca7efb55caf751fec6eef1e16c300548cbf85 -r /usr/bin/runc -b /home/testaccount/.local/share/containers/storage/vfs-containers/6dbd08b69aa189466ff8f248e20ca7efb55caf751fec6eef1e16c300548cbf85/userdata -p /tmp/1000/vfs-containers/6dbd08b69aa189466ff8f248e20ca7efb55caf751fec6eef1e16c300548cbf85/userdata/pidfile -l /home/testaccount/.local/share/containers/storage/vfs-containers/6dbd08b69aa189466ff8f248e20ca7efb55caf751fec6eef1e16c300548cbf85/userdata/ctr.log --exit-dir /run/user/1000/libpod/tmp/exits --conmon-pidfile /home/testaccount/.local/share/containers/storage/vfs-containers/6dbd08b69aa189466ff8f248e20ca7efb55caf751fec6eef1e16c300548cbf85/userdata/conmon.pid --exit-command /usr/bin/podman --exit-command-arg --root --exit-command-arg /home/testaccount/.local/share/containers/storage --exit-command-arg --runroot --exit-command-arg /tmp/1000 --exit-command-arg --log-level --exit-command-arg debug --exit-command-arg --cgroup-manager --exit-command-arg cgroupfs --exit-command-arg --tmpdir --exit-command-arg /run/user/1000/libpod/tmp --exit-command-arg --runtime --exit-command-arg runc --exit-command-arg --storage-driver --exit-command-arg vfs --exit-command-arg container --exit-command-arg cleanup --exit-command-arg 6dbd08b69aa189466ff8f248e20ca7efb55caf751fec6eef1e16c300548cbf85 --socket-dir-path /run/user/1000/libpod/tmp/socket --log-level debug --syslog]
WARN[0000] Failed to add conmon to cgroupfs sandbox cgroup: mkdir /sys/fs/cgroup/systemd/libpod_parent: permission denied 
DEBU[0000] Received container pid: 3984                 
DEBU[0000] Created container 6dbd08b69aa189466ff8f248e20ca7efb55caf751fec6eef1e16c300548cbf85 in OCI runtime 

It hangs till I cancel the command. I've used multiple releases of Podman, Slirp4netns, Conmon, and Runc.

**Describe the results you expected:**
Expected a working container with portmapping.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version 1.2.0-dev
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**


- 4GB RAM
- 32GB HDD

Could you please take a look? Thank you so much.

```

RemoteAPI Version:  1

OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

The `Failed to add conmon to cgroupfs sandbox` line is harmless; we can continue without issue when it happens. I'd actually say it's entirely expected on rootless (we should probably disable actually attempting it in the rootless case).

From the Podman side, it seems like your container has been created and is running. I imagine that, if you hit it with a `podman kill` or `podman stop` from another terminal, it will probably stop the container (I'd be very interested in whether this works). The problem seems to be that the initial Podman process is not exiting.

The fact that this reproduces as both root and rootless is very concerning, though.
Hm. From the positioning of the hang, the cause could be starting the container. If `runc` hangs when we request that it starts the container, that would cause the command we see here.

@michielhemme can you temporarily disable SELinux and see if you see different results?
@henrywang Please check `journalctl` for any AVCs (they'd be formatted like `audit[23984]: AVC avc:  denied  { write } for  pid=12345` in the logs) and paste them here, so we can tell what SELinux is blocking.

> @michielhemme can you temporarily disable SELinux and see if you see different results?
> @henrywang Please check `journalctl` for any AVCs (they'd be formatted like `audit[23984]: AVC avc: denied { write } for pid=12345` in the logs) and paste them here, so we can tell what SELinux is blocking.

Tried it with SELinux, disabled, enforced, permissive. All resulting in a hang after started container (so when runc needs to take over). 

I've also tried to install it in RHEL 8 since it is released now. (Standard package doesn't support rootless port mapping), but I get the same error (freeze at runc). i tried multiple runc rpms and even builded everything from scratch but all resulting to the same thing.

> @michielhemme can you temporarily disable SELinux and see if you see different results?
> @henrywang Please check `journalctl` for any AVCs (they'd be formatted like `audit[23984]: AVC avc: denied { write } for pid=12345` in the logs) and paste them here, so we can tell what SELinux is blocking.

Thanks @mheon! Here's my AVC:
`AVC avc:  denied  { map } for  pid=5713 comm="entry_point.sh" path="/usr/bin/env" dev="fuse" ino=1334900 scontext=system_u:system_r:container_t:s0:c112,c627 tcontext=system_u:object_r:fusefs_t:s0 tclass=file permissive=0`

And my podman ps -a result:
```
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                     COMMAND               CREATED        STATUS                      PORTS  NAMES
9ff5afa03103  docker.io/selenium/hub:3  /opt/bin/entry_po...  3 minutes ago  Exited (139) 3 minutes ago         selenium-hub
```
I have `container-selinux-2.100-1.git3b78187.fc30.noarch` installed.

That is allowed in the current policy, which you indicate is installed.

Could you try

dnf reinstall container-selinux

And see if anything blows up?

Actually my issue isn't with root user. It works flawlessly on root. The only issue I got is that port binding causes this issue on rootless (I can start a container without port binding, but I can only use Podman if port binding works). 

Since RHEL8 contains Podman V1.02-dev doesn't support rootless port bindings, I needed to install a newer RPM. So far I've tried:
Podman 1.3-dev
Podman 1.2
Podman 1.2-dev
Podman 1.12

I've also tried numerous versions of container-selinux, slirp4netns, and runc.
@rhatdan I was wondering if you could give any more insight in the release cycle of Podman within Red Hat 8? For example when will 1.12 arrive?
Currently we have to wait until RHEL8.1 to update container runtimes.  I am trying to change this.

@michielhemme What AVC's are you seeing on Fedora 30?

ausearch -m avc -ts recent

> That is allowed in the current policy, which you indicate is installed.
> 
> Could you try
> 
> dnf reinstall container-selinux
> 
> And see if anything blows up?

@rhatdan, re-install `container-selinux-2.100-1.git3b78187.fc30.noarch`works for me. Appreciate your help! Does that mean we always need re-install `container-selinux` when updating from Fedora 29 to 30? Thanks.
No, your the only one that I heard about this from.  Something went wrong when you were updating or installing.  I will be looking for similar issues, and hope that this was just your issue. :^)
